ksa_text: Conduct comprehensive AI risk assessments to identify and document technical, organizational, and mission-level risks, including threat identification, vulnerability analysis, and risk prioritization.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.

ddat.top_element_label: Applying statistical and analytical tools and techniques

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of intelligence data gathering policies and procedures

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Knowledge of information technology (IT) risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.
Applying statistical and analytical tools and techniques
Knowledge of intelligence data gathering policies and procedures

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA RM-T-001 (RM, Task) does not clear the weak fit inclusion threshold of 0.52. Composite score 0.4674 is insufficient to establish relevance to clinical data management, regulatory compliance, and healthcare analytics at IC-to-senior-IC level. Framework signals (nist_ai_rmf=0.467, eu_ai_act=0.441, dcwf=0.415) do not demonstrate sufficient alignment with core analytics/BI work.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 exclude upheld. Score 0.4674 below threshold for weak fit domain; no rescuing analytics text signals. No internal inconsistencies identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does RM-domain KSA RM-T-001 (composite 0.4674) appear in published data analyst or BI role profiles?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: RM domain content (AI governance, security/privacy, risk management, compliance) is not a primary expectation in IC-to-senior-IC ANL roles per COSO ERM framework; risk competencies in IC ANL scope limited to data risk identification, not enterprise risk management. Composite 0.4674 confirms weak signal. Exclusion consistent with published ANL frameworks.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 exclude upheld. External evidence confirms RM domain KSA at composite 0.4674 is not a standard requirement for Clinical Data Manager at IC-to-senior-IC level.

decision_rationale: Pipeline consensus: exclude. P2 exclude confirmed by P3 (internal) and P4 (external) adversarial agents. Zero overturns. Composite 0.4674, domain RM, scope weak fit. High confidence.
